_comment: Interactive multi-CLI review. Each reviewer is launched as a real interactive CLI in its own WezTerm pane (so you watch it work and it uses its own native code-review skill + tools). The orchestrator sends 'instruction' to the pane via wezterm send-text; the agent then writes its findings JSON to {OUT} using its own file-write tool. Placeholders in 'instruction': {DIFF}=path to the diff, {PROMPT}=path to the criteria+schema file, {OUT}=path the agent must write its JSON findings to. 'launch' must start the CLI in interactive mode with permission prompts disabled (so the file write isn't blocked). 'ready_wait' = seconds to let the CLI boot before sending the instruction. The 'reconciler' runs headless (stdin) to merge all findings into the final review.
instruction: You are a code reviewer. Use your own code-review skill and judgment to review the unified diff in the file {DIFF}. Follow the review criteria and the STRICT JSON output schema described in {PROMPT}. Do NOT post anything to GitHub and do NOT modify any source files. Write ONLY your findings, as a single JSON object matching that schema, to the file {OUT} (create it; overwrite if it exists). Once {OUT} contains valid JSON, your task is complete.
